title: agastache rugosa
intro: Agastache rugosa, also known as the Kori Mikuru or Mexican giant hyssop, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its distinctive purple, pink, or white flowers and mint-like fragrance. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Agastache rugosa has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its ability to soothe respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Agastache rugosa can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
